text: Stepping through glass doors into a space aglow with technological promise, I feel the hum of innovation surround me. The "R + D"—Research and Development—appears as both an altar and a playground. There’s excitement, but also a curious detachment; the boundary between observer and participant blurs. I am both inside and outside the experiment, part of the relentless push to know and to create. Here, curiosity and progress are almost tangible, yet I wonder: do we drive the machines, or do they already beckon us onward, their silent glow promising answers to questions I have not yet dreamed?
